Tittle & Associates And Asero Insurance Services is denied as moot. The parties filed a formal stipulation on May 18, 2015 withdrawing the motion.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Or Limit Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued To National Fire Insurance Company Of Hartford
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. Aldo E. Ibarra of Nixon Peabody LLP (415-984-8233) appeared for plaintiffs, opposing party. Atty. Kevin McLaughlin of Meyers/ Nave (510-808-2000) appeared for defendant San Francisco Unified School District, moving party.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF D.L. FALK CONSTRUCTION INC. Motion To Quash Or Limit Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Issued To National Fire Insurance Company Of Hartford is taken off calendar. On October 21, 2014, the moving party filed a notice of withdrawal of the motion. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Disco
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Atty. Aldo E. Ibarra of Nixon Peabody LLP (415-984-8233) appeared for plaintiffs, opposing party. Atty. Kevin McLaughlin of Meyers/ Nave (510-808-2000) appeared for defendant San Francisco Unified School District, moving party.

Hearing held, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery as to interrogatories and requests for production is to grant in part and deny in part. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anthony Barron, Esq., 415.984.8309, for plaintiff D.L. Falk Construction Inc.
Kevin Gilbert, Esq., 510.808.2000, for defendants San Francisco Unified School District, Maureen Shelton and David Goldin.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MAUREEN SHELTON and DAVID GOLDIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support the claim for punitive damages. The allegations regarding the Monroe Elementary School Modernization Project are somewhat attenuated with respect to the 1350 7th Ave. School project. Those allegations are nevertheless not legally irrelevant for purposes of a motion to strike, since they raise an issue whether defendants' conduct regarding the Monroe project shows malice as to the 7th Ave. project. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anthony Barron, Esq., 415.984.8309, for plaintiff D.L. Falk Construction Inc.
Kevin Gilbert, Esq., 510.808.2000, for defendants San Francisco Unified School District, Maureen Shelton and David Goldin.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MAUREEN SHELTON and DAVID GOLDIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support a claim against the individual defendants under Gov't Code sec. 822.2. Gov't Code sec. 820.2 does not bar the claim because plaintiff's complaint does not describe basic policy/discretionary decisions and Gov't 820.2 carves out statutory liability exceptions. The cause of action is not time barred because Gov't Code sec. 945.6 only applies to public entities. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
